annotations.label: Winding number

anchor.snippet: the winding number or winding index of a closed curve in the plane around a given point is an integer representing the total number of times

annotations.note: A grep for "winding" across Mathlib returns nothing; the winding number is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Winding number as counterclockwise turns

anchor.snippet: the winding number of the curve is equal to the total number of counterclockwise turns that the object makes around the origin

annotations.note: No winding number notion exists in Mathlib, so this intuitive characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Four counterclockwise, one clockwise

anchor.snippet: if the object first circles the origin four times counterclockwise, and then circles the origin once clockwise, then the total winding number of the curve is three

annotations.note: This illustrative additivity example has no formalization since winding numbers are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero and negative winding numbers

anchor.snippet: a curve that does not travel around the origin at all has winding number zero

annotations.note: No winding number is defined in Mathlib, so claims about its sign/zero value are not formalized.

annotations.label: Winding number may be any integer

anchor.snippet: the winding number of a curve may be any integer

annotations.note: Surjectivity of the winding number onto ℤ is not formalized as the concept itself is absent.

annotations.label: Formal definition via lifted path

anchor.snippet: be a continuous closed path on the complex plane minus one point

annotations.note: The lifting machinery used by the definition (path lifting through the covering map `Circle.isCoveringMap_exp`) exists in Mathlib, but the winding number itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Winding number is well defined

anchor.snippet: The winding number is well defined because of the existence and uniqueness of the lifted path

annotations.note: Existence and uniqueness of the lifted path is formalized (`IsCoveringMap.exists_path_lifts`/`liftPath` and `eq_liftPath_iff`), though the winding-number well-definedness conclusion it supports is not stated.

annotations.label: Integer because path is closed

anchor.snippet: It is an integer because the path is closed

annotations.note: The integrality of the winding number for a closed path is not formalized since the winding number is undefined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alexander numbering rule

anchor.snippet: A simple combinatorial rule for defining the winding number

annotations.note: The combinatorial Alexander-numbering rule has no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equal winding number within a region

anchor.snippet: The winding numbers of the curve around two points in the same region are equal

annotations.note: Constancy of the winding number on a region is not formalized; no winding-number object exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero in unbounded region

anchor.snippet: The winding number around (any point in) the unbounded region is zero

annotations.note: Vanishing of the winding number on the unbounded component is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjacent regions differ by one

anchor.snippet: the winding numbers for any two adjacent regions differ by exactly 1

annotations.note: The jump-by-one rule across an arc is not formalized since winding numbers are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Winding number in complex coordinates

anchor.snippet: can be expressed in terms of the complex coordinate z = x + iy

annotations.note: Mathlib has the circle integral ∮(z−w)⁻¹=2πi (`circleIntegral.integral_sub_inv_of_mem_ball`) but no general definition of the winding number as a contour integral over an arbitrary curve.

annotations.label: Index of a point with respect to a curve

anchor.snippet: also known as the index of

annotations.note: The index of a point relative to a closed curve is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Special case of Cauchy integral formula

anchor.snippet: This is a special case of the famous Cauchy integral formula

annotations.note: The Cauchy integral formula (of which the winding-number integral is the f≡1 special case) is fully formalized, though only over circles, not arbitrary curves.

annotations.label: Properties of the winding number

anchor.snippet: constant over each component (i.e., maximal connected subset)

annotations.note: Locally-constant and integrality properties of the winding number are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Winding number of a circular path

anchor.snippet: is the path defined by

annotations.note: Mathlib proves ∮_{C(c,R)}(z−w)⁻¹ dz = 2πi for w inside, which is exactly the winding number 1 of a circular path, but it is not phrased in winding-number terms.

annotations.label: Winding number as degree of a mapping

anchor.snippet: the winding number is an alternate term for the degree of a continuous mapping

annotations.note: Mathlib has no topological degree of continuous self-maps of the circle/spheres.

annotations.label: Topological quantum numbers

anchor.snippet: winding numbers are frequently called topological quantum numbers

annotations.note: This physics terminology has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complement of a point is homotopy equivalent to circle

anchor.snippet: The complement of a point in the plane is homotopy equivalent to the circle

annotations.note: Mathlib defines homotopy equivalence (`HomotopyEquiv`) but I found no theorem that the punctured plane is homotopy equivalent to the circle.

annotations.label: Maps homotopic to standard maps

anchor.snippet: each such map can be continuously deformed to (is homotopic to) one of the standard maps

annotations.note: Classification of self-maps of the circle up to homotopy by z↦zⁿ is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental group

anchor.snippet: The set of homotopy classes of maps from a circle to a topological space form a group

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` is defined (with a `Group` instance) as homotopy classes of loops, matching the definition of the fundamental group.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of the circle is Z

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of the circle is the additive group of the integers

annotations.note: π₁(S¹)=ℤ is listed only as a TODO example in `Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/HomotopyGroup.lean` and is not proved.

annotations.label: Maps from the 3-sphere classified by an integer

anchor.snippet: Maps from the 3-sphere to itself are also classified by an integer

annotations.note: Degree classification of S³→S³ maps (π₃(S³)=ℤ) is not formalized; it is only a TODO note in HomotopyGroup.lean.

annotations.label: Winding number with respect to the tangent

anchor.snippet: the winding number of the path with respect to the tangent of the path itself

annotations.note: The turning number defined via the tangent direction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Turning number of the introductory curve

anchor.snippet: has a winding number of 3, because the small loop is counted

annotations.note: This specific turning-number example has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Degree of the tangential Gauss map

anchor.snippet: is the degree of the tangential Gauss map

annotations.note: Neither the tangential Gauss map nor its degree is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Turning number and total curvature

anchor.snippet: This is called the turning number

annotations.note: A grep for total/signed curvature and turning number finds nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polygon density

anchor.snippet: the turning number is referred to as the polygon density

annotations.note: Polygon density (turning number of a polygon) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Density of simple polygons is 1

anchor.snippet: the density is 1, by the Jordan curve theorem

annotations.note: The Jordan curve theorem is not in Mathlib (only unrelated 'Jordan' results), and polygon density is undefined.

annotations.label: Density of a star polygon

anchor.snippet: the density is q

annotations.note: Density of star polygons {p/q} is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Turning number undefined for space curves

anchor.snippet: Turning number cannot be defined for space curves as degree requires matching dimensions

annotations.note: This remark about space curves has no formalization since turning number/degree theory is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent turning sign

anchor.snippet: for locally convex , closed space curves , one can define tangent turning sign as

annotations.note: The tangent turning sign of locally convex space curves is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two non-degenerate homotopy classes

anchor.snippet: Its two values correspond to the two non-degenerate homotopy classes of locally convex curves

annotations.note: The classification of locally convex curves into two non-degenerate homotopy classes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Solutions classified by winding number

anchor.section: Winding number and Heisenberg ferromagnet equations
anchor.snippet: Solutions of the last equations are classified by the winding number or topological charge

annotations.note: The Heisenberg ferromagnet equations and their topological-charge classification are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Point in polygon via winding number

anchor.snippet: A point's winding number with respect to a polygon can be used to solve the point in polygon

annotations.note: The point-in-polygon algorithm via winding number is not formalized in Mathlib.
